mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals an abrupt labor market shock that triggers corporate margin compression and equity risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.5pp threshold over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of real residential construction starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates synchronized housing and consumption demand recovery, which expands corporate revenue growth expectations and lifts S&P 500 valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power if the joint rank correlation between HOUST and RSAFS changes fails to explain more than 5% of the variance in 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive decades.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, sustained industrial production growth reflects robust capacity utilization that translates directly into higher corporate operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month window.
failure_condition: The mechanism is rejected if INDPRO month-over-month changes show zero correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns during periods where the NBER recession dating committee classifies the economy as late-cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP consistently outpaces the long-term trend rate, signaling expanding aggregate demand that improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the historical probability of positive 5-day SPX returns following above-trend GDP prints falls below 52% over a 30-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers anticipatory Federal Reserve tightening expectations, which raises real interest rates and compresses equity duration premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The claim fails if 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive in over 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, regardless of subsequent Fed policy actions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which reduces wage-price spiral fears and supports sustained equity risk appetite, lifting S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect is invalidated if a multivariate regression of PAYEMS and UNRATE changes yields an adjusted R-squared below 0.02 for 20-day forward SPX returns over any 15-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recovery regime, monthly housing starts consistently exceeding 1.3 million units signal strong builder confidence and downstream consumption linkages, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as financials and industrials outperform.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations where HOUST surpasses 1300 thousand units while GDPC1 growth remains below 2.0% annualized.
